labs.title: Quick Demo: ISPF Primary Menu
labs.description: 30-second automated walkthrough. Connect, authenticate, reach ISPF.

labs.title: Session Stack: Trust Boundary Mapping
labs.description: Map the VTAM → TSO → ISPF session layers. Identify where identity is bound and trust shifts.

labs.title: Dataset Model: PDS, Members & Catalogs
labs.description: Enumerate catalog entries and dataset attributes. Map the storage namespace and trust-sensitive datasets.

labs.title: Deferred Execution: JCL → JES
labs.description: Submit a job and trace the execution chain. Identity binding, JES scheduling, deferred trust.

labs.title: RACF: Authorization Plane Analysis
labs.description: Examine how RACF evaluates access. Resource profiles, identity footprint, policy enforcement gaps.

labs.title: RAKF Security: Users, Profiles & Privilege Analysis
labs.description: Explore the RAKF security system on TK5. View users and profiles tables, understand access control, identify misconfigurations, and trace authorization decisions.

labs.title: System Inspection: Configuration & Procedures
labs.description: Inspect system datasets, PROCLIB, and configuration. Map what a normal user can see.

labs.title: Address Space Anatomy
labs.description: Inspect address spaces, allocations, and job output. Destroy the process-centric mental model.

labs.title: COBOL Compile & Execute on MVS
labs.description: Create a COBOL program, write JCL, submit the compile-link-go job. Trace the deferred execution model.

labs.title: CICS/KICKS: Transaction Processing
labs.description: Map the transaction execution plane. Install and run CICS transactions on the TK5 lab.

labs.title: JCL Library Injection: Privilege Path
labs.description: Demonstrate how a low-privileged user influences batch execution by controlling a program library. No exploits — just normal MVS behavior.

labs.title: APF Privilege Escalation: MODESET to Superuser
labs.description: The classic mainframe privesc. Find writable APF library, assemble MODESET program, escalate to SPECIAL+OPERATIONS. Based on mainframed/APFPRIV.jcl.

labs.title: System Enumeration: z/OS Pentest Recon
labs.description: Enumerate APF libraries, RACF settings, SVC table, TSO auth tables, logged-on users. Based on mainframed/ENUM_REFACTORED.

labs.title: RACF Hash Cracking: DES Password Analysis
labs.description: How RACF password hashing works — DES + EBCDIC. Why the algorithm is weak and how to crack hashes. Based on mainframed/user_hashes.py.

labs.title: Z/OS Reverse Shell via JCL (Metasploit)
labs.description: Analyze the reverse_shell_jcl payload. Trace deferred execution, identity binding, and the submission-to-execution gap.

labs.title: DC30 Lab 1: Run HELLO — First Mainframe Program
labs.description: Log in to TSO, learn ISPF navigation, and run your first mainframe program. The HELLO binary uses gets() — a deliberately vulnerable C function. Foundation for all buffer overflow labs.

labs.title: DC30 Labs 2–4: Buffer Overflow on MVS
labs.description: Overflow the HELLO program's 150-byte gets() buffer, find the return address in a dump, use De Bruijn patterns to pinpoint the offset, and control execution to print a WTO message. Classic stack overflow — on a mainframe from 1978.

labs.title: DC30 Lab 9: ARBAUTH Privilege Escalation — Follow the White Rabbit
labs.description: Use ARBAUTH to run code as AC=1, exploit the OPENTST buffer overflow to modify your ACEE security control block in memory, escalate privileges, and read the protected WHITE.RABBIT dataset.

labs.title: DC30 Lab 10: FTP Buffer Overflow — Remote Code Execution
labs.description: Exploit a buffer overflow in the MVS FTP server via a long USER command. Use De Bruijn patterns, craft EBCDIC-safe shellcode, and achieve remote code execution on the mainframe over the network — no TSO session required.
